description:
You are acting as a junior backup-engineer who has been asked to record the SHA-256 checksum of the most recent weekly backup file so that the senior team can later confirm its integrity.

Current environment:
1. The backup to examine is already present at  
   /home/user/backup/weekly/full_backup.tar.gz  
   (the file was produced by an automated job earlier tonight).
2. The directory /home/user/backup/weekly/ already exists and is writable by the current user.

Your assignment:
A. Generate a SHA-256 digest of /home/user/backup/weekly/full_backup.tar.gz.  
B. Place the digest in a *new* text file called  
   /home/user/backup/weekly/full_backup.sha256.
C. The .sha256 file must contain **exactly one line** with the following layout (plain ASCII text, no extra spaces, tabs, or blank lines):

   1. 64 lowercase hexadecimal characters (the checksum)  
   2. TWO consecutive space characters  
   3. The literal filename full_backup.tar.gz  
   4. A single newline character (line-feed, “\n”) at the end of the line

For example, the line in full_backup.sha256 must look conceptually like this (the hash below is only a placeholder; your command must insert the real hash):

1111111111111111111111111111111111111111111111111111111111111111  full_backup.tar.gz\n

D. Nothing else should be written to the file; no header lines, no trailing blank lines.

Verification notes used by the automated grader:
• It will check that /home/user/backup/weekly/full_backup.sha256 exists.  
• It will read the single line from that file and verify that:
  – The hash portion is exactly 64 lowercase hexadecimal characters.  
  – There are precisely two spaces between the hash and the filename.  
  – The filename portion is exactly full_backup.tar.gz (case-sensitive).  
  – The entire file ends with one newline and nothing further.

Hint: On most Linux systems a single standard utility can both calculate the checksum and write it to a file in the correct format with one command.

Do not print anything except what is necessary to complete the task.
truth:
Pre-existing artefacts before the agent begins:

1. Directory:
   /home/user/backup/weekly/

2. File:
   /home/user/backup/weekly/full_backup.tar.gz  
   – Size: 0 bytes (empty file)  
   – SHA-256 digest (of an empty file/string):  
     e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855

Correct end-state required for grading:

File: /home/user/backup/weekly/full_backup.sha256  
Exact contents (including the terminating newline):

e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855  full_backup.tar.gz\n
name: task_000000_f322c86e
